formData.problemDesc:
From July to December 2025, Tapes/Adhesives was the top offender for Defect Tier 2 in Cable Assy.
Tapes/Adhesives Rejection rate at 1.1% affecting the internal acceptability.
Tapes/Adhesives COPQ was $3,238 representing 0.043%.
36%(6 of 17) employees from All PR lines aging was less than 1 year. 
AE5/AE5N the top offender with 66.66% defects contribution
50%(3 of 6) employees from AE5/AE5N aging was less than 1 year.
formData.objective:
To Improve Tapes/Adhesives rate from 1.1% reject rate to 0.55% by the end of June 2026
Improve Internal Acceptability rate from 2025 avg of 91.9% to 92.5% by the end of June 2026
Improve Tapes/Adhesives COPQ rate to 0.022% by the end of June 2026
formData.businessBenefit: Reduce the rejection rate and COPQ rate by 50% by the end of June 2026, that would benefit AERO internal acceptability and COPQ rate.

whyChains.problem: The application of Tapes/Adhesives as top offender for Defect Tier 2 in Cable Assy, affected internal acceptability by 0.88% in H2’25?
whys: the assemblies were being built incorrectly.
the assemblies were built with Para-Aramid (Kevlar) and fiber glass tape with incorrect lengths
the assemblers uses their tribal knowledge for length tolerances, including when a breakout or final termination is present on the drawing pattern.
they considered that this is a faster way to built the assemblies and do not waste their time reviewing customer AVs
the cables are blocking visually the cross-hatch patter on form board .
whyChains.rootCause: The cables are blocking visually the cross-hatch pattern on form board.

formData.paretoNotes:
The departments AE5/N, AE6/N, and SPX have a 70.7% contribution, representing 58 NCRs created during that period. 
Also, a different analysis was conducted to review the main contributors per material and application process that failed using the NCRs from the period of July to October, and see 2nd image of table

Based on the previous table, it was concluded that the main contributors were “Para-Amid Tape (Kevlar)” & “Insultherm” under the classification “Length.”

solutions.title: Create Visual Aid and Visual Improvement to formboards

activities.what: Create a list of all the visual improvements that the foamboard needs to reduce the issues related to tape/adhesives.
activities.benefit: Clarifying the expected benefits by making quality standards easier to identify

activities.what: Define a color coding for those new improvements, to standardize the Improvements on all PNs.
activities.benefit: Establish a consistent, standardized approach across all production departments to eliminate confusion regarding formboard patterns

activities.what: Identify into the foamboards for the 92 RMs (HF/HS) the areas that need to be incorporated the visual improvement suggested.
activities.benefit: Enhance harness production efficiency by increasing output and reducing NCR occurrences

activities.what: Initiate manual markup of formboard in AE2 department
activities.benefit: Provide a clear understanding of customer requirements and quality standards

activities.what: Monitor and receive feedback of formboard markup
activities.benefit: Making sure the manual markup works and is helpful to the assemblers.

activities.what: Create a Master VA for reference for tapes and adhesives
activities.benefit: Improve efficiency by reducing time spent navigating specifications and simplifying comprehension during the assembly process

activities.what: Initiate manual markup of formboard in XXX department
activities.benefit: Provide a clear understanding of customer requirements and quality standards

activities.what: Train employees on the formboard, VA and other updates
activities.benefit: Boost KPI performance and build stronger assembler capability through improved experience and knowledge

activities.what: Monitoring the results of the NCRs related to Tapes/Adhesives for the initial 92 RMs with the improvements, then the rest of the RMs based on the OOR.
activities.benefit: Improve traceability by enabling clearer identification of issues arising from incorrect operations, such as tape and adhesive NCRs
